current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
architecture_findings: round 5: factorized_topk likely failed because its hidden mixer is too narrow and lossy; widening that stage and adding a gate should preserve more information before latent selection and materially improve FVU. Re-test factorized_topk at K=128 after this single architectural stabilization edit. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 6: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal. -> incubate (fvu=0.3485000617802143, observed_fvu=0.348134, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 7: A hybrid encoder that preserves the strong linear topk path and adds a gated nonlinear residual to latent logits can improve quality without discarding the baseline behavior that already works. Test a new hybrid_topk family at K=128 as a quality-first architecture probe. -> keep (fvu=0.18129383772611618, observed_fvu=0.177086, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 10: hybrid_topk may now be limited by using the nonlinear branch only as an additive correction; letting it multiplicatively modulate the linear logits should improve support shaping while preserving the strong baseline path. Re-test hybrid_topk at the best-known K=128, expansion_factor=8, optimizer=adam setting after this targeted hybrid-family stabilization edit. -> discard (fvu=0.27785650826990604, observed_fvu=0.278638, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 14: The current best family may need cleaner support suppression rather than another additive or multiplicative residual path. An adaptive-threshold TopK encoder that keeps the proven linear logits as the anchor but learns an input-dependent nonnegative per-latent suppression term could reduce false activations at K=128 and materially improve FVU without abandoning the strong baseline initialization. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 16: The recent hybrid and adaptive-threshold results suggest the main failure mode is not lack of an anchor but how nonlinear information is injected into support selection. A union_topk encoder that keeps the baseline linear top-k proposal intact while letting a nonlinear branch propose an alternate candidate set, with final support chosen from the union rather than from perturbed logits, could lower FVU by adding complementary features without destabilizing the proven linear path. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 17: The union_topk prototype likely failed because its nonlinear branch proposed candidates from the same centered input as the linear branch, so it mostly duplicated baseline support. Conditioning the nonlinear proposer on the linear branch residual, widening that branch, and letting it over-propose candidates before the final global top-k should increase support complementarity at K=128 and lower proxy FVU. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 18: Recent architecture edits have mostly tried to improve support selection, yet multiple distinct families have converged to the same proxy FVU plateau under normal runtime. A stronger next bet is that support is not the main bottleneck at K=128; instead, the selected coefficients are too crude. A refined_topk family that keeps the proven linear top-k support but recomputes the chosen coefficients with a small per-example ridge solve against the decoder atoms could materially lower FVU by directly reducing reconstruction error on the selected support. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 19: The round-18 refined_topk crash was caused by a mixed-precision implementation bug in the ridge solve, not by evidence against the architecture. Running the small support-wise solve in float32 and casting the refined coefficients back to the model dtype should make the family trainable under bf16 autocast, allowing a valid proxy read on whether coefficient refitting can lower FVU at K=128. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

failure_patterns.pattern: factorized_topk likely failed because its hidden mixer is too narrow and lossy; widening that stage and adding a gate should preserve more information before latent selection and materially improve FVU. Re-test factorized_topk at K=128 after this single architectural stabilization edit.

Recent architecture edits have mostly tried to improve support selection, yet multiple distinct families have converged to the same proxy FVU plateau under normal runtime. A stronger next bet is that support is not the main bottleneck at K=128; instead, the selected coefficients are too crude. A refined_topk family that keeps the proven linear top-k support but recomputes the chosen coefficients with a small per-example ridge solve against the decoder atoms could materially lower FVU by directly reducing reconstruction error on the selected support.

The round-18 refined_topk crash was caused by a mixed-precision implementation bug in the ridge solve, not by evidence against the architecture. Running the small support-wise solve in float32 and casting the refined coefficients back to the model dtype should make the family trainable under bf16 autocast, allowing a valid proxy read on whether coefficient refitting can lower FVU at K=128.

topk.design_hypothesis: Because the frontier is empty, the next highest-value move is to establish a trustworthy K=128 quality anchor with the simplest mainline architecture before rewarding smaller K or drawing conclusions about new families. Run a proxy topk baseline at K=128 with the default expansion and optimizer explicitly pinned so later rounds can measure real FVU gains against a stable reference.
next_steps: If this anchor is stable, escalate immediately to a higher-capacity or more expressive encoder family that could materially beat its FVU at the same K=128.
If the anchor quality is already much better than expected, then test quality-first upgrades such as mixture/factorized/residual encoders before considering smaller K.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
next_steps: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal.
next_steps: If the fixed factorized_topk now yields a valid proxy improvement, keep incubating the family with quality-focused tuning.
If the fixed factorized_topk still performs poorly under normal runtime, stop spending rounds on current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder design.

hybrid_topk.design_hypothesis: Recent new families have all clustered far above the current hybrid full frontier, so the most credible next move is a training-dynamics pass on the one family that has actually improved full FVU. Adding a modest orthogonality penalty to hybrid_topk may reduce decoder interference between active features, sharpen the effective support at K=128, and improve reconstruction quality without changing the proven architecture.
next_steps: If hybrid_topk with ortho regularization improves proxy FVU materially, promote it to full as the next mainline quality test.
If the ortho pass is flat or worse, bias the next round toward a more expressive new architecture edit rather than more local regularization sweeps.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: jumprelu looks promising enough to deserve a family-specific stabilization pass, and its current default threshold initialization is likely too weak to realize the benefit of learned gating. Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam using a higher initial threshold and smoother STE bandwidth so the encoder starts from a more selective regime.
next_steps: If stabilized jumprelu materially improves proxy FVU, promote it to full before spending more rounds on hybrid variants.
If stabilized jumprelu is flat or worse, keep hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 as mainline and either try another new family or return to a training-dynamics experiment on the hybrid checkpoint.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE may materially lower proxy FVU at K=128 by decoupling support selection from coefficient magnitude, letting one branch learn which features should fire while another learns how strongly they should reconstruct. After two flat jumprelu stabilization rounds and stalled hybrid local search, this is the cleanest untried quality-first family already supported by the execution layer.
next_steps: If gated shows a strong proxy signal, promote it to full before returning to smaller-K or cost work.
If gated is also weak, bias the next round toward another genuinely different encoder family or a code edit for a new architecture rather than more parameter-only tuning on recent families.

adaptive_threshold_topk.design_hypothesis: The current best family may need cleaner support suppression rather than another additive or multiplicative residual path. An adaptive-threshold TopK encoder that keeps the proven linear logits as the anchor but learns an input-dependent nonnegative per-latent suppression term could reduce false activations at K=128 and materially improve FVU without abandoning the strong baseline initialization.
next_steps: If adaptive_threshold_topk shows a strong proxy gain, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If adaptive_threshold_topk is flat or weak, the next round should bias toward a more expressive new architecture or a hybrid-family training-dynamics experiment rather than revisiting gated or jumprelu local sweeps.

union_topk.design_hypothesis: The union_topk prototype likely failed because its nonlinear branch proposed candidates from the same centered input as the linear branch, so it mostly duplicated baseline support. Conditioning the nonlinear proposer on the linear branch residual, widening that branch, and letting it over-propose candidates before the final global top-k should increase support complementarity at K=128 and lower proxy FVU.
next_steps: If residual-conditioned union_topk materially improves proxy FVU, promote this family toward mainline comparison and consider a full run.

If it remains near the recent 0.275 proxy plateau under normal runtime, pivot away from branch-union variants to a more radical encoder family rather than spending more rounds on local union_topk tuning.

refined_topk.design_hypothesis: The round-18 refined_topk crash was caused by a mixed-precision implementation bug in the ridge solve, not by evidence against the architecture. Running the small support-wise solve in float32 and casting the refined coefficients back to the model dtype should make the family trainable under bf16 autocast, allowing a valid proxy read on whether coefficient refitting can lower FVU at K=128.
next_steps: If the fixed refined_topk yields a meaningful proxy FVU gain, follow with a family-specific stabilization pass on regularization strength or support source before considering promotion.

If refined_topk now runs normally but lands near the recent 0.275 proxy plateau, conclude that coefficient refitting alone is insufficient and pivot to a genuinely different encoder family next round.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: hybrid_topk has now beaten the K=128 full baseline, so the next quality-first move is to stabilize the same family with an optimizer better suited to its smooth nonlinear residual branch. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam to see whether denser adaptive updates improve FVU beyond the current full frontier.

hybrid_topk with Adam is now the strongest K=128 configuration, so the next quality-first move is to increase representational capacity rather than shrink K or chase cost. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam and expansion_factor=16 to see whether extra latent capacity can materially reduce FVU again.

hybrid_topk may now be limited by using the nonlinear branch only as an additive correction; letting it multiplicatively modulate the linear logits should improve support shaping while preserving the strong baseline path. Re-test hybrid_topk at the best-known K=128, expansion_factor=8, optimizer=adam setting after this targeted hybrid-family stabilization edit.

Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam using a higher initial threshold and smoother STE bandwidth so the encoder starts from a more selective regime.

recent_insights: round 7: A hybrid encoder that preserves the strong linear topk path and adds a gated nonlinear residual to latent logits can improve quality without discarding the baseline behavior that already works. Test a new hybrid_topk family at K=128 as a quality-first architecture probe. -> keep (fvu=0.18129383772611618, observed_fvu=0.177086, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
hybrid_topk is now the best full K=128 frontier at FVU 0.18129383772611618, narrowly beating the previous topk anchor 0.18532327562570572.
Round 8 keeps focus on hybrid_topk and tests whether Adam can better optimize its nonlinear residual branch than signum.

round 8: hybrid_topk has now beaten the K=128 full baseline, so the next quality-first move is to stabilize the same family with an optimizer better suited to its smooth nonlinear residual branch. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam to see whether denser adaptive updates improve FVU beyond the current full frontier. -> keep (fvu=0.16189537942409515, observed_fvu=0.156928, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
hybrid_topk plus Adam improved the full K=128 frontier to FVU 0.16189537942409515 with no runtime regression relative to the prior hybrid run.
Round 9 escalates capacity within the leading family by testing expansion_factor=16 at fixed K=128 and Adam before considering any lower-K or cost-driven moves.

round 9: hybrid_topk with Adam is now the strongest K=128 configuration, so the next quality-first move is to increase representational capacity rather than shrink K or chase cost. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam and expansion_factor=16 to see whether extra latent capacity can materially reduce FVU again. -> archive (fvu=0.1623645443469286, observed_fvu=0.156466, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

hybrid_topk with Adam and expansion_factor=16 achieved better proxy FVU but slightly worse full FVU (0.1623645443469286 vs 0.16189537942409515) and a clear throughput regression, so brute-force scaling is not the next priority.

Round 10 stabilizes hybrid_topk by adding a zero-initialized multiplicative modulation path on top of the existing additive nonlinear residual, keeping the model anchored to the proven linear encoder at initialization.

round 10: hybrid_topk may now be limited by using the nonlinear branch only as an additive correction; letting it multiplicatively modulate the linear logits should improve support shaping while preserving the strong baseline path. Re-test hybrid_topk at the best-known K=128, expansion_factor=8, optimizer=adam setting after this targeted hybrid-family stabilization edit. -> discard (fvu=0.27785650826990604, observed_fvu=0.278638, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
The best current full frontier remains hybrid_topk with Adam at K=128, expansion_factor=8, FVU 0.16189537942409515.
Round 11 reopens architecture exploration after hybrid local edits stalled, using jumprelu as a new family with learned thresholding at fixed K=128.

round 11: A thresholded sparse activation family may beat the current hybrid mainline by learning which latent logits should be suppressed before top-k selection rather than only reshaping logits with extra branches. Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam as a fresh quality-first family probe. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
jumprelu at K=128/ef8/adam reached proxy FVU 0.2749013416469097, which is modestly competitive with recent hybrid proxy points but not yet enough for promotion.
Round 12 performs the first family-specific jumprelu stabilization pass by increasing the initial threshold and STE bandwidth to encourage more meaningful learned gating.

round 12: jumprelu looks promising enough to deserve a family-specific stabilization pass, and its current default threshold initialization is likely too weak to realize the benefit of learned gating. Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam using a higher initial threshold and smoother STE bandwidth so the encoder starts from a more selective regime. -> incubate (fvu=0.27477459236979485, observed_fvu=0.275135, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
Round 13 opens the previously untested gated family as a quality-first K=128 proxy probe after jumprelu threshold tuning remained flat around 0.275 proxy FVU.
The gated architecture decouples latent support selection from activation magnitude, which is a substantively different encoder bias from hybrid additive correction and jumprelu thresholding.

Round 14 adds a new adaptive_threshold_topk family that preserves the plain linear TopK encoder at initialization and learns only input-dependent nonnegative suppression of latent logits.
A quick local import/forward sanity check passed for adaptive_threshold_topk before handing control back to the runtime.

Adaptive_threshold_topk did not improve beyond the recent jumprelu-level proxy plateau, so support-suppression alone is not enough to beat the current hybrid mainline.
Round 15 returns to hybrid_topk for a training-dynamics probe, testing whether mild decoder orthogonality regularization can improve K=128 quality by reducing active-feature interference.

round 15: Recent new families have all clustered far above the current hybrid full frontier, so the most credible next move is a training-dynamics pass on the one family that has actually improved full FVU. Adding a modest orthogonality penalty to hybrid_topk may reduce decoder interference between active features, sharpen the effective support at K=128, and improve reconstruction quality without changing the proven architecture. -> discard (fvu=0.2779793106019497, observed_fvu=0.278651, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
Round 16 adds a new union_topk family that preserves the linear TopK proposal and lets a nonlinear branch contribute only through alternate candidate proposals before the final global top-k.
A quick local import and forward check passed for union_topk before returning control to the runtime.

round 16: The recent hybrid and adaptive-threshold results suggest the main failure mode is not lack of an anchor but how nonlinear information is injected into support selection. A union_topk encoder that keeps the baseline linear top-k proposal intact while letting a nonlinear branch propose an alternate candidate set, with final support chosen from the union rather than from perturbed logits, could lower FVU by adding complementary features without destabilizing the proven linear path. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
Round 17 stabilizes union_topk by making the nonlinear proposal branch residual-conditioned on the linear partial reconstruction instead of reading the same centered input.

The union_topk stabilization also widens the nonlinear branch and lets it over-propose 2k candidates before the final global top-k, aiming to improve candidate diversity without changing decode semantics.
A local import/forward/backward sanity check passed for the edited union_topk implementation before handing control back to the runtime.

round 17: The union_topk prototype likely failed because its nonlinear branch proposed candidates from the same centered input as the linear branch, so it mostly duplicated baseline support. Conditioning the nonlinear proposer on the linear branch residual, widening that branch, and letting it over-propose candidates before the final global top-k should increase support complementarity at K=128 and lower proxy FVU. -> incubate (fvu=0.2749013416469097, observed_fvu=0.275225, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
Round 18 adds a new refined_topk family that keeps the baseline linear top-k support but refits the selected coefficients with a small ridge solve against the decoder atoms.
The refined_topk implementation uses a straight-through blend so the forward path uses refined coefficients while preserving gradient flow through the baseline top-k activations.
A local import/forward/backward sanity check passed for refined_topk before handing control back to the runtime.

Round 19 fixes a refined_topk mixed-precision bug: the support-wise ridge solve now runs in float32 and casts refined coefficients back to the model dtype.
The refined_topk crash in round 18 came from a bf16/float32 mismatch inside torch.linalg.solve under autocast, so that round should not be treated as architecture evidence.
A local bf16 import/forward/backward sanity check passed for the fixed refined_topk implementation before handing control back to the runtime.
